title: podman's docker-compose support sets incorrect CreateCommand which breaks systemd generation
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman sets the incorrect CreateCommand info on containers created through docker-compose, which breaks systemd unit file generation.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a container with docker-compose. E.g.:
```
version: "3"
services:
  terraria:
    restart: always
    image: docker.io/ryshe/terraria:latest
    command: --log-opt max-size=200k
    container_name: terraria
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '1.0'
          memory: 4G
    ports:
      - 7777-7778:7777-7778
    environment:
      WORLD_FILENAME: "Terrarius.wld"
    volumes:
      - ./world:/root/.local/share/Terraria/Worlds
    networks:
      - terraria

networks:
  terraria:
    name: terraria_default
```

2.  run container with `sudo docker-compose up -d`

3. Inspect container with: `sudo podman inspect --format '{{.Config.CreateCommand}}' terraria`

**Describe the results you received:**

The CreateCommand is `[/usr/bin/podman --log-level=info system service]`, and `sudo podman generate systemd --new --name terraria` fails with an error that the create command is too short or invalid.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Mon Aug  2 12:39:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical machine.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @andrew-kennedy!

That's a known but, to be fair, not-well documented limitation.  `podman generate systemd **--new**` requires the container to be created via Podman.  Containers created via the remote/REST API, which is the case when using `docker-compose`, do not work.

Background: `generate systemd --new` is looking at the "create command" of the container which is simply the `os.Args` at container creation.

Unfortunately, I don't see a way to support that since there's no way we can reverse-map an existing container (without a proper "create command") to command-line flags and options.

I will open a PR though to make this more obvious in the man pages.

@vrothberg thank you for the info and the documentation updates! This does lead me to a question though: is there no official way to do declarative podman image configuration and then systemd unit generation? With docker I could create a set of compose files that I could update and add to as needed for all the services on a system. With podman, I figured I could leverage docker-compose API support to match this using the systemd unit file generation, but it seems this might not be the case?

I would like to avoid maintaining a set of bash `podman create` scripts as my set of "declarative" podman images.
